verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a member of the EGF family synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (~170 aa) anchored at the plasma membrane before ectodomain shedding by ADAM proteases releases the mature soluble ~46 aa peptide. The TM-anchored proform is transiently present on the cell surface and mediates juxtacrine signaling; however, the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble ligand that binds EGFR/ERBB4 on neighboring cells. The TM precursor stage is real but short-lived, placing EREG in the transient-TM-proligand category — contextual/dual_localization rather than yes.
